title: Lasianthaea squarrosa
taxonomy: Lasianthaea squarrosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Lasianthaea, which is part of the daisy family. It is a native species found in the Americas, specifically in the southwestern United States and Mexico. Further classification includes its order (Asterales), class (Magnoliopsida), and division (Magnoliophyta). This plant is considered a rare and unique species due to its limited geographic range.

intro: Lasianthaea squarrosa, also known as the Mexican cigar plant or Lasianthaea, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthaea squarrosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lasianthaea is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
